title: Add .fleet sidebar route + FleetView.swift binding FoundryFleet to substrate registry vault
description: Foundry has substantial existing architecture (4 sidebar routes: Workspace/discovery, Dependency Graph, Status, Operator Queue). FoundryAppLib now depends on SubstrateRegistryVault + SubstrateWorkspaceVault and exposes a `FoundryFleet @MainActor ObservableObject` facade with 11 typed query methods. The 30-min next-session task: (1) add `case fleet` (or `.registry` / `.catalog` per operator preference) to `FoundrySidebarRoute` enum in `FoundryContentView.swift`, (2) add corresponding label (e.g., 'Fleet') + SF Symbol icon, (3) create `FleetView.swift` that `@StateObject var fleet = FoundryFleet()` + `.onAppear { fleet.load(monoRoot: ...) }` + a SwiftUI `List(fleet.aliasedRegistryEntries) { entry in ... }` rendering display name + hash badge + category + install status, (4) wire the new route in `FoundryContentView`'s switch over selected route. Complementary to existing `.discovery` route: discovery walks disk live (FoundryDiscoveryModel); fleet renders typed canonical registry (FoundryFleet). Both views, different angles on the same substrate. After this lands, foundry has its first vault-backed view — the substrate's first really-visible artifact from this session's work.
